text:
Market today: Risk-off Friday. The real driver is a tech rout — Micron down 6% premarket signals chip names are getting hit hard, and the first U.S. stock outflow since March hints institutional money is stepping back. Your concentrated, leveraged book feels this more than most.

Your holdings: PLTR (-15.85%) is flagged SELL — already deep in the red, momentum is not your friend here. AVGO (-14.78%) is your largest position and flagged TRIM — a chip-sector down day makes this more urgent, not less. SPAL (-19.44%) is a 2x leveraged ETF on a brand-new IPO with almost no price history — that combination in a risk-off tape is a fast way to get stopped out; it's already near its $19 stop. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: (1) How SMH trades today — it's your only green chip name and tells you how bad the sector damage really is. (2) Whether AVGO holds above its $336 stop.

Bottom line: Protect capital first — trim or cut the losers before margin turns a bad day into a catastrophic one.
